claim_statement: The Sachs-Wolfe plateau (ell 2-30) in Planck TT is suppressed by factor f_SW = d_s^ΛCDM / d_s^QNG = 4/4.082 = 0.9799 relative to ΛCDM, a parameter-free ~2% deficit.
dataset: Planck CMB TT power spectrum (R3.01, ell 2-30)
method: 1) Compute mean SW plateau: Delta_SW = mean(ell*(ell+1)*C_ell / (2*pi)) for ell in [2, 30]. 2) Compare to ΛCDM expected plateau amplitude (from Planck 2018 best-fit parameters). 3) Compute ratio: f_SW^obs = Delta_SW^Planck / Delta_SW^ΛCDM. 4) Compare to QNG prediction f_SW = 0.9799 +/- propagated d_s uncertainty. 5) Note: cosmic variance sigma_CV ~ 30% at ell=10, so result is indicative only.
pass_condition: Observed f_SW^obs within 1-sigma (cosmic variance dominated) of predicted 0.9799 AND directional agreement (f_SW^obs < 1, i.e., deficit not excess).
fail_condition: Observed f_SW^obs > 1.05 (excess rather than deficit) or f_SW^obs < 0.85 (deficit too large to be explained by d_s alone).

parameters.note: Cosmic variance dominates at low ell; test is directional, not precision. Consistent with known Planck low-ell power deficit.

decision.note: f_SW_obs = 0.823 < 1.0 (deficit, directional PASS). 0.615 sigma from QNG prediction 0.980. PASS.
